channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Boda Boda Rider's Body Found in River After 3 Weeks Missing
item.description: James Gichohi Wanjau, a boda boda rider from Githumu Village in Murang'a County, was reported missing on 28 June 2026. His mutilated body was allegedly recovered from a river, with his head found sepa...

item.title: DOJ assures NBI's professional investigation into SEA Games infrastructure
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has assured the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) will conduct a thorough and impartial probe into allegations of infrastructural irregularities during the 2019 So...

item.title: Global CKD Diagnosis Crisis: Over 400 Million Cases Remain Undiagnosed, Warns Lancet Study
item.description: A recent study published in The Lancet reveals that nearly half of the estimated 844 million chronic kidney disease (CKD) cases worldwide remain undiagnosed, with higher rates in low- and middle-incom...

item.title: Jozi FM Governance Dispute Escalates as Legal Battle Over Board Control Intensifies
item.description: A legal battle over the governance of Jozi FM has reignited in the Gauteng High Court, with director Rapitse Peter Montsho challenging the authority of a rival board to manage the community radio stat...

item.title: Pakistan Urges US and Iran to Resume Talks to Ease Regional Tensions
item.description: Pakistan's Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Hussain Andrabi has emphasized that the country will continue to encourage the United States and Iran to stop violence and resume technical-level talks und...

item.title: Alberta Court Extends Asset Freeze on Separatist Lawyer in Legal Dispute with First Nation
item.description: A Alberta judge has extended and expanded a temporary order freezing the assets of Jeff Rath, a leader of the province’s separatist movement, in a legal dispute with the Tallcree First Nation. The ord...

item.title: Nasir El-Rufai's 150-day Detention Sparks Concern Over Lawfare in Nigeria
item.description: The article discusses the prolonged detention of Nasir El-Rufai, a prominent Nigerian politician and critic of President Tinubu, who has been held for 150 days. His wife, Ahmad El-Rufai, highlights th...

item.title: Ontario adopts unified regulatory framework for public companies
item.description: Ontario has finalized its agreement to join Canada's 'passport' system for publicly traded companies, marking the end of its long-standing push for a national securities regulator. This decision resol...

item.title: Tuchel Refuses to Blame Curse After England's Semi-Final Loss to Argentina
item.description: England's football team faced another setback in their World Cup campaign as they lost to Argentina in the semi-finals, extending their wait for a major trophy. Manager Thomas Tuchel addressed the def...

item.title: Sudbury Launches Youth Mental Health Program This Fall
item.description: A new youth mental health initiative is set to launch in Sudbury, Ontario, this fall, aiming to address rising concerns about adolescent well-being in the region. The program, spearheaded by local hea...

item.title: Celebrating the Legacy of Top Gun: A Look at the Cast's Journey Over Time
item.description: The article explores the careers of the actors from the 1986 film 'Top Gun,' highlighting their journeys post-film. It details how some stars like Tom Cruise and Val Kilmer remained prominent in Holly...

item.title: Ancient rock carvings in Delhi's Central Ridge reveal pre-Ashokan history
item.description: Archaeologists have discovered cup-like depressions and linear markings on a rock in Delhi's Central Ridge forest, potentially dating back to the pre-Ashokan era. These findings, noticed by naturalist...

item.title: England and South Africa Cricket Coaches Face Major Changes Amid Leadership Shifts
item.description: The cricket coaching landscape is undergoing significant upheaval as England and the SA20 tournament grapple with leadership changes. Brendon McCullum's sacking as England's Test coach and Stephen Fle...

item.title: Journalist's Home Subjected to Alleged Arson Attempt in Laguna
item.description: A journalist in Laguna, Philippines, reported an incident where their residence was allegedly targeted by a fire. The event occurred early Thursday morning when the homeowner and their spouse woke up ...

item.title: CAC Launches Sixth Batch of Company Deregistration, Aims to Remove 100,000 Non-Compliant Firms
item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has initiated its sixth batch of deregistering companies that failed to meet statutory obligations, putting 100,000 firms at risk of removal from Nigeria's corpo...

item.title: Spain Advances to World Cup Final After Defeating France
item.description: Madrid erupted in celebration as Spain secured a 2-0 victory over France in the FIFA World Cup semi-final, sending them to the final. Thousands of fans filled central Madrid, waving the red-and-yellow...

item.title: Boda boda Riders Demand Justice After Chairman's Alleged Murder in Kawangware
item.description: Boda boda operators in Dagoretti North staged a peaceful protest demanding justice for Thomas Kitheka, the chairman of Congo Stage boda boda riders who was allegedly murdered. The riders marched throu...

item.title: Africa Needs to Reform Healthcare Training
item.description: African nations are facing growing pressure to overhaul their healthcare training systems due to severe shortages of qualified medical professionals and rising disease burdens. Current training models...

item.title: Sindh CM addresses financial challenges and infrastructure projects
item.description: Sindh Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah stated that the allocation for provinces under the National Finance Commission (NFC) Award has decreased this year due to provinces contributing part of their share...

item.title: JMPD officers rescue abandoned newborn baby in Johannesburg
item.description: On July 16, 2026, Johannesburg Metropolitan Police Department (JMPD) officers from the Rea Vaya Unit rescued an abandoned newborn baby girl in the inner city. The incident occurred around 11:45pm when...
